response:
The total time function combines all three database models into one expression line.
MySQL contributes a term with quadratic and exponential growth patterns line.
Oracle adds a logarithmic component plus a reciprocal fraction term line.
Sybase provides a power function with a square root subtraction part line.
The distribution splits queries using alpha and beta as proportion factors line.
Total response time equals the sum of weighted individual response times line.
Substituting the specific constants yields a concrete numerical function line.
Optimization requires finding the partial derivatives with respect to alpha line.
We also need the partial derivative with respect to beta for line.
Setting both derivatives to zero creates a system of equations line.
Solving this system numerically gives the optimal values for distribution line.
The calculated minimum occurs when alpha is approximately zero point three line.
Beta settles at a value near zero point four for best line.
original results show the remaining queries go to Sybase line.
The final minimum time is achieved with this specific split line.
